**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 544

An academy gives your citizens—and the PCs themselves— an institution where advanced study in many fields can be pursued, researched, and referenced.

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** 2

**Cost** 52 RP, 12 Lumber, 6 Luxuries, 12 Stone
</row>

**Construction**
[Scholarship](/Skills.aspx?ID=29) (expert) DC 27

**Upgrade From**
[Library](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=28)

**Upgrade To**
[Military Academy](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=36), [University](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=70)

**Item Bonus**
+2 item bonus to [Creative Solution](/Actions.aspx?ID=1403)

**Effects**
While in a settlement with an Academy, you gain a +2 item bonus to Lore checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=516), to all checks made while [Researching](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205), and to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22).

</column>

<title level="1" right="Kingdom Structure 15" pfs="">[Cathedral](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Building" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=442" />
<trait label="Edifice" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=445" />
<trait label="Famous" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=447" />
<trait label="Infamous" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=448" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 545

A cathedral serves as a focal point of spiritual worship in the settlement and the seat of regional power for a religion. Most cathedrals are astounding works of art and eye-catching marvels of architecture.

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** 4

**Cost** 58 RP, 20 Lumber, 20 Stone
</row>

**Construction**
[Folklore](/Skills.aspx?ID=24) (master) DC 34

**Upgrade From**
[Temple](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=64)

**Item Bonus**
+3 item bonus to [Celebrate Holiday](/Actions.aspx?ID=1395), [Provide Care](/Actions.aspx?ID=1389), and [Repair Reputation](/Actions.aspx?ID=1381) (Corruption)

**Effects**
The first time you build a cathedral in a turn, reduce Unrest by 4. While in a settlement with a cathedral, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516), and to all faith-themed checks made while [Researching](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205). Treat the settlement's level as three levels higher than its actual level for the purposes of determining what [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) magic items are available for sale in that settlement. This effect does not stack with the similar effect granted by shrines or temples.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Kingdom Structure 1" pfs="">[Cemetery](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Yard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=443" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 545

A cemetery sets aside a plot of land to bury the dead and can also include above-ground vaults or underground catacombs.

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** 1

**Cost** 4 RP, 1 Stone
</row>

**Construction**
[Folklore](/Skills.aspx?ID=24) DC 15

**Effects**
Giving the citizens a place to bury and remember their departed loved ones helps to temper Unrest gained from [dangerous](/Traits.aspx?ID=450) events. If you have at least one cemetery in a settlement, reduce Unrest gained from any dangerous settlement events in that particular settlement by 1 (to a maximum of 4 for four cemeteries). The presence of a cemetery provides additional effects during certain kingdom events.

</column>

A guildhall serves as the headquarters for a trade guild or similar organization. It includes offices for its leaders and functionaries as well as workshops for its craftspeople and a storefront for customers. Guildhalls always specialize in a certain type of trade or pursuit, but typically, only the largest cities have multiple guildhalls. Smaller settlements tend to focus on one particular trade.

**Upgrade From**
[Trade Shop](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=69)

**Item Bonus**
+1 item bonus to Economy skill checks associated with the guildhall's specific trade focus

**Effects**
When you build a guildhall, indicate what sort of organization (such as bakers, grocers, smiths, etc.) it serves as a headquarters for. While in a settlement with a guildhall, you gain a +1 item bonus to all related skill checks to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) or to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42).

</column>

<traits>
<trait label="Infrastructure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=444" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 545

Bridges give settlements that have water borders a connection to land (but at the GM's option, a border on a lake might not be able to use bridges).

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** —

**Cost** 6 RP, 1 Lumber or 1 Stone
</row>

**Construction**
[Engineering](/Skills.aspx?ID=22) DC 16

**Effects**
A bridge allows an island settlement to provide influence (see [Influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1830)), negates the Trade penalty for island settlements (see [Land Borders](/Rules.aspx?ID=1821)), and allows travel over its associated Water Border with ease (see [Navigating an Urban Grid](/Rules.aspx?ID=1822)). Bridges can only be built on Water Borders. When you build a bridge, check the “Bridge” box on one of the Water Borders on your Urban Grid to indicate its location.

</column>
